category.name: Lists
category.preferred_workflow: Use Aspose.Words List and ListFormat APIs through DocumentBuilder with explicit list assignment, explicit nested levels, and RemoveNumbers after completion.

strict_api_bans: Do not invent unsupported list APIs.
Do not skip assigning ListFormat.List before writing list items.
Do not forget to remove list formatting.
canonical_patterns: Apply the list through builder.ListFormat.List before writing list items.
Use builder.ListFormat.ListLevelNumber for nested list levels.
Call builder.ListFormat.RemoveNumbers() after finishing a list.
nullable_warning_prevention: Avoid maybe-null dereference when reusing list references.
Guard document nodes before reading list-related properties.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Code uses unsupported helpers such as CreateList or AddListItem.
Paragraphs are written but they are not actual list items.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Assign builder.ListFormat.List before writing each list sequence.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Nested items appear at the wrong level or never return to the parent level.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Set builder.ListFormat.ListLevelNumber explicitly before each nested level transition.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Subsequent paragraphs continue as list items unintentionally.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Call builder.ListFormat.RemoveNumbers() immediately after the list is complete.

examples.task: Create a default numbered list in a Word document using DocumentBuilder.ListFormat.ApplyNumberDefault method.
Apply a default bulleted list to paragraphs with DocumentBuilder.ListFormat.ApplyBulletDefault method for formatting.
Set ListLevelNumber to three before adding a paragraph to create a third-level list item in the document.
Increase list indent programmatically using DocumentBuilder.ListFormat.IncreaseIndent to move paragraph deeper within the current list structure.
Decrease list indent with DocumentBuilder.ListFormat.DecreaseIndent to promote paragraph to higher level in the outline.
Remove numbering from a paragraph by calling DocumentBuilder.ListFormat.RemoveNumbers method to stop list formatting.
Create a custom List object, configure its levels, and add it to Document.Lists collection.
Add a new List to the document's ListCollection and assign it to multiple paragraphs to share formatting.
Assign an existing List to a paragraph by setting Paragraph.ListFormat.List property to apply the desired list style.
Clone an existing List, modify its level start values, and apply the cloned list to new sections.
Set NumberStyle of a ListLevel to UpperRoman to generate Roman numeral list items.
Apply a custom bullet character to a list by setting ListLevel.NumberStyle to Bullet and defining BulletChar.
Define StartingNumber for a ListLevel to begin numbering at five instead of default one.
Restart numbering for each new section by resetting ListLevel.StartingNumber before applying the list.
Create a numbered list that restarts numbering at each new chapter by resetting StartingNumber on chapter start.
Create a nine-level hierarchical list by defining properties for each ListLevel up to level nine.
Create a multi-level list with alternating bullet and number styles by configuring each ListLevel individually.
Adjust ListLevel indentation by setting ListLevel.Indentation to 36 points for proper alignment.
Apply a custom tab stop to list levels to align text after numbers using ListLevel.TabPosition property.
Configure TabPosition of a ListLevel to 72 points to align text after list number.
Validate that each list in a document does not exceed nine levels to comply with API constraints.
Implement error handling for attempts to create more than nine list levels, catching the resulting exception.
Ensure OoxmlCompliance is set higher than Ecma376 before saving document to retain custom list settings.
Check document OoxmlCompliance property and set to higher than Ecma376 when modifying list definitions.
Retrieve a specific List from Document.Lists by its ID and adjust its level properties programmatically.
Iterate through Document.Lists collection to modify all list definitions uniformly across the document.
Apply a uniform list style to all lists by iterating over Document.Lists and updating each level.
Programmatically increase list level depth for a paragraph using DocumentBuilder.ListFormat.IncreaseIndent within a loop.
Programmatically decrease list level depth for a paragraph using DocumentBuilder.ListFormat.DecreaseIndent inside a conditional.
Programmatically remove list formatting from selected paragraphs while preserving their indentation using ListFormat.RemoveNumbers.
